question: After a closed 5-minute BTC shock, does an altcoin's contemporaneous underreaction relative to its ex-ante BTC beta predict same-direction BTC-neutral residual from the next bar open through the following 15 minutes?
holdout_release_rule: Download evaluation only if every predictive development gate passes and primary mean is at least 12 bps; download confirmation only if the unchanged evaluation gate passes. Never revise economic rules after release.
known_exposure: Prior Halpha studies viewed some daily or 2-hour history for these instruments, but no result from this question's 5-minute beta-gap event definition was viewed before this checkpoint.
search_disclosure: 15 fixed alts; one primary configuration; seven one-at-a-time sensitivities; two shock signs; half-year splits; two directional baselines; per-asset exploratory BY-FDR.
allowed_fixes_after_result_release: official CSV header or timestamp parsing
implementation defects that do not change symbols, periods, signal, target, baselines, sensitivities, costs or gates
study_code_revision_reason: Before any analysis or 5-minute result was viewed, the first source preparation exposed two transient HTTP IncompleteRead failures after 238 of 240 files passed checksum. The only change adds IncompleteRead to the existing three-attempt download retry list; symbols, periods, signal, target, baselines, sensitivities, costs and gates are unchanged.
